search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae constipation telehealth history red flags investigation referral 2024
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic laxative, stool softener, diet, imaging, or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Constipation follow-up requires actual response and secondary-cause context.
No treatment or investigation is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: DHA telehealth constipation population; children, pregnancy, postoperative, neurological, opioid-related, and suspected obstruction contexts require separate evidence.
setting_applicability: Primary, gastroenterology, hepatology, colorectal, result-review, or outpatient follow-up as qualified by the exact source.
UAE_applicability: International evidence requires UAE gastroenterology, colorectal, hepatology, laboratory, endoscopy, referral, prescribing, screening, and local-pathway adaptation.
recency_verification: Exact official documents and sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The cited official source remains current on the issuing organisation website at the review date.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact DHA history section supports interval stool frequency and form, straining, incomplete evacuation, abdominal symptoms, bleeding, medicines, and impact without assigning a cause.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies nature, onset, duration, bowel diary, secondary causes, straining, incomplete evacuation, abdominal pain, bleeding, and medication context.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact red-flag section supports clinician assessment and concern documentation without populating negatives.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists new onset in older people, anaemia, rectal bleeding, family history, tenesmus, weight loss, and persistent unresponsive constipation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact section supports recording already reviewed investigations in persistent or suspected-secondary contexts.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Limits laboratory and colorectal imaging evaluation to persistent, unresponsive, or suspected secondary disease.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact section supports a clinician-entered safety net, follow-up, and referral status without generating urgency.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists emergency bleeding and specialist referral criteria for constipation.

unresolved_source_gaps: Current bowel pattern, response, adherence, secondary cause, examination, diagnosis, medicine, dose, investigation, referral, and interval remain unsupported.
